title: hemarthria coromandelina
intro: Hemarthria coromandelina, also known as Indian cup grass or Coromandel button grass, is a tropical grass native to India and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and treating skin infections. Hemarthria coromandelina can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for use in traditional medicine.
